trial_details.trajectory:
Okay, let's diagnose and fix the problematic Kubernetes pod.The pod is in an ErrImageNeverPull status, which means the Docker image could not be pulled.The issue is that the pod is trying to pull an image named "misspeling-app:latset", which seems to have a typo in the image name. The image name should be "misspelling-app:latest".The patch command updated the image name for the container in the deployment to "misspelling-app:latest", which should resolve the ErrImageNeverPull issue.

FIX COMPLETE

The diagnosis was that the pod was unable to pull the image due to a typo in the image name. I fixed this by patching the deployment to use the correct image name "misspelling-app:latest".

## COMMANDS EXECUTED:
1. $ kubectl get pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-h5lzd -n case6-test
   Output: NAME                                      READY   STATUS              RESTARTS   AGE
misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-h5lzd   0/1     ErrImageNeverPull   0          13s...
2. $ kubectl describe pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-h5lzd -n case6-test
   Output: Name:             misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-h5lzd
Namespace:        case6-test
Priority:         0
Service Account:  default
Node:             minikube/192.168.49.2
Start Time:       Mon, 15 D...
3. $ kubectl patch deployment misspelling-deployment -n case6-test --type=json -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/image","value":"misspelling-app:latest"}]'
   Output: deployment.apps/misspelling-deployment patched...
